tweet: That's all the #Veterans Day facts for today! To learn more:  http://bit.ly/bVOKsv Thank you to all our brave veterans!

name: Archive: Senator McCaskill Office
tweet: Fact #5: It wasn’t until 1978 when the gov’t began observing the #Veterans Day we know today.

name: Archive: Senator McCaskill Office
tweet: Fact #5: On Sept. 20, 1975 Pres. Ford signed a law which returned the annual observance of #Veterans Day to the original date of Nov. 11.

name: Archive: Senator McCaskill Office
tweet: Fact #4: In 1958, the Uniform Holiday Bill moved #Veterans Day to a Monday in Nov. Many did not agree and continued to practice on Nov. 11

name: Archive: Senator McCaskill Office
tweet: Fact #3: In 1938, Nov 11 was officially made a legal holiday, but at the time it was to be celebrated and known as "Armistice Day” #Veterans

name: Archive: Senator McCaskill Office
tweet: Fact #2: In November 1919, President Wilson declared Nov. 11 as the first commemoration of Armistice Day. #Veterans

name: Archive: Senator McCaskill Office
tweet: Veterans Day falls on Nov 11 every year b/c WWI armistice fell on this day in 1918. It was regarded as the end of “the war to end all wars.”

name: Archive: Senator McCaskill Office
tweet: As we observe Veterans Day tomorrow, here's a few facts as to why Nov. 11 is the day we honor our brave #veterans.
